statement: The pinned repository builds from a clean checkout.
proof_strategy: Run lake build in clean CI; record toolchain hashes.
stop_condition: Build fails or toolchain is not reproducible.
notes: This is the first gate; all REPORTED_EXISTING nodes remain unverified until it passes.

statement: Every theorem claimed as banked is imported and #checked by one audit module.
proof_strategy: Generate an import-only audit file with #check declarations and compile it.
stop_condition: Any named declaration is absent, renamed, or convention-incompatible.
notes: Separates user-reported banked results from locally verified results.

statement: Public capstones contain no sorry/admit and depend only on an approved axiom whitelist.
proof_strategy: Scan sources; add #print axioms checks for capstones; fail CI on forbidden tokens.
stop_condition: A public theorem depends on an undeclared axiom or sorry.
notes: Classical choice may be allowed; physics assumptions must be named structures, not axioms.

statement: One canonical (+---) convention package for Minkowski vectors, Pauli maps, adjoints, and natural units.
proof_strategy: Wrap existing definitions; prove conversion lemmas; deprecate duplicates.
stop_condition: Two trusted modules require incompatible signatures or normalizations.
notes: Do not redefine existing project types unless reconciliation is impossible.

statement: Linear equivalences preserving the Minkowski form and future orientation.
proof_strategy: Define as a linear equivalence plus form-preservation and future-cone preservation fields.
stop_condition: The definition cannot compose cleanly or act on all later fibers.
notes: Avoid committing to a full Lie-group manifold in the finite spine.

statement: Pluecker scalars, Hermitian momenta, self-dual curvature spinors, and spacetime bivectors remain distinct types.
proof_strategy: Use separate namespaces/types; provide only explicit equivalences and coercions.
stop_condition: A theorem silently coerces between inequivalent representations.
notes: This is an API rule enforced by review rather than one mathematical theorem.

statement: For unit future-timelike U, normalized future-null k are equivalent to unit spacelike xi orthogonal to U via k=U+xi.
proof_strategy: Expand Minkowski squares and inner products in both directions.
stop_condition: Future-directedness of U+xi cannot be proved from the chosen predicates.
notes: Split algebraic nullity from strict future-directedness if needed.

statement: The six rest-space directions ±e1, ±e2, ±e3 with uniform weights.
proof_strategy: Define by Fin 6 table in the standard rest frame.
stop_condition: Coordinate definitions create excessive simplification cost.
notes: Use rational coordinates and dedicated simp lemmas.

statement: The octahedral null fan has mean U and isotropic second moment in the rest frame.
assumptions: U is the standard rest vector
proof_strategy: Finite sum normalization; symmetry cancellation; direct coordinates.
stop_condition: Normalization or factor 1/3 disagrees with conventions.
notes: Add tetrahedral design only after this exact rational pilot is stable.

statement: A finite probability law on normalized future-null vectors with prescribed barycenter U.
assumptions: [Fintype Omega]; PMF Omega; U unit future timelike
proof_strategy: Use PMF rather than raw real weights; store nullity, normalization, and barycenter proofs.
stop_condition: PMF API makes vector expectation cumbersome.
notes: A raw-weight wrapper may exist privately, but public APIs should use probability objects.

statement: The octahedral design instantiates FiniteNullResolution for the rest vector.
proof_strategy: Package existing moment and nullity lemmas.
stop_condition: Packaging exposes a convention mismatch.

statement: A probability measure on NullFiber U whose Bochner first moment is U.
proof_strategy: Store probability, integrability, and barycenter fields.
stop_condition: The identity map on the subtype is awkward to integrate.
notes: This abstraction lets all flux theorems avoid depending on the sphere construction.

statement: Observer flux-reweights a finite null resolution by (n·k)/(n·U).
assumptions: n and U unit future timelike; positive inner products
proof_strategy: Define a PMF after proving nonnegativity and total mass one.
stop_condition: Strict positivity lemmas for future causal vectors are missing.
notes: Prove cone inner-product lemmas once in Lorentz4.

statement: The flux-weighted mean observer direction equals U/(n·U)-n.
proof_strategy: Cancel the flux denominator using the barycenter field; no coordinate integration.
stop_condition: Observer direction normalization conventions differ.
notes: This is the key flux theorem and should precede continuum measure work.

statement: The flux variance of unit directions is 1/(n·U)^2.
assumptions: KIN-006; every observer direction has norm one
proof_strategy: Use E||omega-beta||^2 = E||omega||^2-||E omega||^2.
stop_condition: Rest-space norm is not aligned with Minkowski conventions.

statement: For p=mU and E=n·p, flux variance equals m^2/E^2 and matches the normalized visible determinant.
proof_strategy: Algebraic substitution; compose with existing determinant wrapper.
stop_condition: Energy normalization in the banked theorem differs by a factor of two.
notes: Record exact trace-versus-energy convention in the theorem name/docstring.

statement: Under flux weighting, E[1/(n·k)] = 1/(n·U).
proof_strategy: The flux factor cancels pointwise, leaving total intrinsic weight.
stop_condition: A zero denominator is possible under weak future predicates.
notes: Keep expectation theorem separate from pathwise clock convergence.

statement: A probability law with an epsilon uniform component has mean norm at most 1-epsilon.
assumptions: Directions have unit norm and uniform component has zero mean
proof_strategy: Convex decomposition and triangle inequality.
stop_condition: None; this is a robust guardrail.
notes: Formalize finite support first; general measure version is optional.

statement: A unit-sphere probability law with mean norm one is concentrated on one direction.
proof_strategy: Equality case of triangle inequality/strict convexity.
stop_condition: Equality characterization becomes disproportionately hard.
notes: A weaker pairwise-equality theorem is acceptable for Gate G1.

statement: A compiling spike constructs normalized measure on S^2 and proves antipodal symmetry and coordinate integrability.
proof_strategy: Write a minimal isolated file before designing the public API.
mathlib_support: Sphere manifold; Haar/volume measure; Bochner integral
stop_condition: The required normalized measure or symmetry API is absent.
notes: If blocked, use a compact-group quotient or explicit spherical-coordinate measure as a fallback.

statement: The pushforward of normalized rest-sphere measure is a probability measure on NullFiber U.
proof_strategy: Pushforward probability under measurable equivalence.
stop_condition: The abstract rest-space sphere is too expensive to instantiate.
notes: Fallback: define first in standard rest frame, then transport by a chosen Lorentz map.

statement: The intrinsic null measure has first moment U.
proof_strategy: Reduce to zero mean of the rest-sphere coordinate map by antipodal symmetry.
stop_condition: Antipodal symmetry does not simplify the vector integral cleanly.
notes: Prove a reusable odd-integral-over-symmetric-measure lemma.

statement: Proper orthochronous Lorentz maps push intrinsic measure at U to intrinsic measure at Lambda U.
proof_strategy: Show Lorentz maps intertwine rest-sphere constructions and preserve normalized measure.
stop_condition: The chosen intrinsic measure construction is not canonical enough for covariance.
notes: Not on the Gate G1 critical path.

statement: Flux reweighting of any NullBarycentricMeasure is a probability measure.
proof_strategy: Use withDensity and the barycenter identity.
stop_condition: ENNReal/Real coercions dominate the proof.
notes: Prove a generic reweighting lemma once, then instantiate.

statement: The continuum flux-weighted direction mean equals the observer relative velocity.
proof_strategy: Mirror KIN-006 with integral linearity.
stop_condition: Observer-direction map fails at a null set due to denominator formulation.
notes: Use positivity of n·k on future null support.

statement: Continuum flux variance equals m^2/E^2.
proof_strategy: Integral version of KIN-007 and KIN-008.
stop_condition: None once MEA-006 is complete.

statement: In a laboratory frame the flux density is (1-beta^2)^2/[4pi(1-beta·omega)^3].
proof_strategy: Compute Radon-Nikodym derivative under aberration; verify normalization by invariant theorem.
stop_condition: Coordinate proof consumes effort without unlocking later theorems.
notes: Treat as a validation theorem, not a dependency of the invariant construction.

statement: A finite-state discrete-time kernel alpha -> PMF beta with composition and pushforward.
proof_strategy: Use functions into PMF and define bind-based composition.
stop_condition: Existing project already has an incompatible stochastic-matrix API.
notes: Provide equivalence to matrices only when needed for linear algebra.

statement: A continuous-time finite jump generator with nonnegative off-diagonal rates and zero row sums.
proof_strategy: Use a rate matrix plus structural proofs; fix row/column convention in module docstring.
stop_condition: Sign convention drifts between Bell current and master equation.
notes: Add a one-page convention test with a two-state example.

statement: Pushing a PMF through composed finite kernels equals successive pushforwards.
proof_strategy: Monad laws / finite sums.
stop_condition: None.
notes: Core reusable lemma for all discrete-time equivariance proofs.

statement: A finite jump generator preserves total probability under the forward equation.
proof_strategy: Swap finite sums and use row-sum zero.
stop_condition: Row/column convention error.

statement: A Weyl two-spinor current is future causal and null; it is strictly future-directed when the spinor is nonzero.
proof_strategy: Use the Pauli/Bloch norm identity; split zero and nonzero cases.
mathlib_support: Complex inner product; banked spin-coherent lemmas
stop_condition: Current normalization differs from banked projector theorem.
notes: Prefer one theorem parametrized by chirality sign plus right/left wrappers.

statement: Abstract two-sector densities, currents, and signed transfer F satisfying opposite continuity equations.
assumptions: A chosen discrete or continuous base space
proof_strategy: Separate local algebra from PDE/discrete divergence implementation.
stop_condition: The abstraction is too PDE-specific to reuse for lattice coins.
notes: Provide discrete-time and continuous-time adapters.

statement: Positive-part rates reproduce the signed transfer current exactly.
proof_strategy: Case split on sign of F; safe division lemmas.
stop_condition: Zero-density division is left implicit.
notes: Add explicit source-zero lemmas before defining rates.

statement: For a positive density derived from amplitudes, signed probability flow out of a zero-density source is zero.
assumptions: Transfer current is a bilinear amplitude expression
proof_strategy: Use Cauchy-Schwarz or amplitude vanishing.
stop_condition: Abstract continuity data do not imply the claim.
notes: Keep this as an instantiation theorem, not a field of the abstract interface unless explicitly assumed.

statement: The Weyl form of the Dirac equation gives opposite chiral transfer F=2m Im(phi_R†phi_L).
proof_strategy: Formalize product-rule continuity calculation after a PDE solution interface exists.
stop_condition: PDE formalization swamps the Bohmian project.
notes: An abstract theorem assuming the Weyl equations is sufficient before constructing solution spaces.

statement: For two probability vectors on {L,R}, the minimum off-diagonal transport coupling is unique and explicit.
proof_strategy: Two-case proof using Delta=pR_prime-pR.
stop_condition: None.
notes: This reusable lemma should drive the checkerboard coin beable.

statement: The minimal two-state coupling defines a valid hidden transition kernel through the local coin.
proof_strategy: Use norm preservation to establish equal total Born weight.
stop_condition: Banked coin convention differs from the proposed update order.

statement: Coin hidden update followed by conditional shift pushes the actual distribution to the next Born distribution.
proof_strategy: Compose target-marginal theorem with deterministic shift.
stop_condition: Position/index shifts are represented inconsistently.
notes: Prove an n-step corollary by induction only after one-step theorem is clean.

statement: Every realized lattice spacetime increment has Minkowski square zero and speed c in lattice units.
assumptions: a=c tau; natural units in core
proof_strategy: Direct coordinate calculation.
stop_condition: Unit restoration is mixed into the core theorem.
notes: Keep core in c=1 and add a units wrapper later.

statement: The hidden lattice process is Born-equivariant for every finite number of ticks.
proof_strategy: Induction using STO-001.
stop_condition: None.

statement: The 1+1 walk obeys cos(E tau)=cos(p tau)cos(m tau).
proof_strategy: Compute trace/determinant or eigenvalue characteristic polynomial.
stop_condition: Different step ordering changes the formula.
notes: Freeze the unitary order in the operator charter.

statement: The joint law rho(z) pi_z(omega) over base state and hidden null direction.
proof_strategy: Define by PMF bind or normalized nonnegative weights.
stop_condition: Conditional laws are not normalized at zero base mass.
notes: Use PMF conditionals independent of rho so zero base mass is harmless.

statement: Any finite zero-sum residual G admits an explicit antisymmetric current with divergence G.
assumptions: Finite nonempty direction type; sum G=0
proof_strategy: Use positive/negative parts and normalize by total surplus; handle zero residual separately.
stop_condition: Division by zero in the zero-residual case.
notes: Define current by if totalSurplus=0 then 0 else formula.

statement: Positive-part rates derived from an antisymmetric current reproduce its divergence.
proof_strategy: Finite Bell-current algebra.
stop_condition: Current may leave a zero-density source.
notes: Either prove compatibility from construction or include it as a named hypothesis.

statement: Base evolution plus angular residual rates preserve the prescribed extended law.
assumptions: A differentiable finite base law satisfying its forward equation
proof_strategy: Expand derivative of rho*pi and cancel residual by the angular master equation.
stop_condition: Discrete-time and continuous-time formulations are conflated.
notes: Provide separate theorems for kernels and jump generators.

statement: A finite incidence matrix B, positive conductances W, target density f, and zero-sum source G.
proof_strategy: Use an oriented edge type and incidence matrix; fix gauge by zero-mean potentials.
stop_condition: Graph abstraction creates more overhead than matrix proof.
notes: Prove matrix theorem first; add graph wrapper later.

statement: For a connected positive-weight graph, the weighted Laplacian kernel is exactly the constants.
proof_strategy: Energy identity plus connectedness propagation.
stop_condition: The chosen directed orientation obscures symmetric energy.

statement: Every zero-sum source lies in the range of the connected weighted Laplacian.
proof_strategy: Finite-dimensional orthogonal-complement/rank-nullity argument.
stop_condition: Range/orthogonal-complement APIs are awkward.
notes: Could solve by deleting one row/column and proving invertibility as fallback.

statement: The gradient flow solving the weighted Poisson equation uniquely minimizes kinetic energy among flows with divergence -G.
proof_strategy: Complete the square or use orthogonal decomposition into gradient plus divergence-free flow.
mathlib_support: Inner product projection; Lagrange multiplier finite algebra
stop_condition: The objective uses the wrong density/conductance weighting.
notes: State the exact energy convention in the charter and test on a 3-node graph.

statement: The minimum-energy antisymmetric flow induces valid jump rates preserving the target finite law.
proof_strategy: Take positive parts and divide by source density.
stop_condition: Target law has zeros.
notes: Zero support should be removed or handled componentwise; do not hide it in division conventions.

statement: A sequence of finite Markov kernels determines a probability measure on infinite trajectories.
assumptions: Measurable finite state spaces; Markov kernels
proof_strategy: Wrap mathlib Ionescu-Tulcea trajectory measure for finite kernels.
stop_condition: The local FiniteKernel API does not coerce smoothly to measurable kernels.
notes: This theorem makes “actual history” a genuine random variable rather than only a master equation.

statement: For i.i.d. integrable null increments with mean U, the empirical mean converges almost surely to U.
proof_strategy: Instantiate the existing Banach-valued strong law.
stop_condition: Measurability/integrability wrappers for finite-valued variables are verbose.
notes: This is the first pathwise coarse-timelike theorem and should be on the critical path.

statement: Positions formed by summing i.i.d. null increments have asymptotic four-velocity U almost surely.
proof_strategy: Rewrite displacement as partial sum of increments.
stop_condition: Indexing convention creates an off-by-one issue.

statement: Observer-time unit-speed directions sampled from the finite flux law have empirical velocity beta.
proof_strategy: Apply strong law in the observer rest space.
stop_condition: The rest-space subtype lacks convenient normed-space instances.
notes: A coordinate version in R^3 is acceptable for the first endpoint.

statement: The empirical average of ds/dt converges almost surely to 1/(n·U).
proof_strategy: Apply scalar strong law to 1/(n·k).
stop_condition: Integrability fails if positivity assumptions are too weak.

statement: P=(1-r)I+rPi preserves the target direction law.
proof_strategy: Direct PMF calculation.
stop_condition: None.

statement: On mean-zero observables, the refresh Markov operator acts by multiplication by 1-r.
proof_strategy: Expand operator action and use zero mean.
stop_condition: Operator orientation mismatch.

statement: The exact absolute spectral gap of the refresh chain is r for 0<r<=1.
proof_strategy: Explicit eigenspace decomposition into constants and mean-zero functions.
mathlib_gap: spectral API optional; algebraic proof easier
stop_condition: Calling it a spectral gap requires a precise project definition.
notes: Define the gap operationally via L2 contraction before eigenvalue language.

statement: Stationary direction correlations decay as (1-r)^n.
proof_strategy: Conditional expectation iteration.
stop_condition: Initial law is not stationary.
notes: State both conditional and stationary versions.

statement: Empirical averages for a finite irreducible aperiodic Markov chain converge almost surely to the stationary mean.
assumptions: Finite chain; invariant law; irreducibility; aperiodicity
proof_strategy: Choose a proof route: regeneration/Doeblin coupling, shift ergodic theorem, or martingale Poisson equation.
mathlib_gap: no turnkey finite Markov SLLN identified
stop_condition: The theorem balloons into a general probability-library project.
notes: Not required for the first complete model; the i.i.d. endpoint already proves the ontology claim.

statement: A certificate that a Markov operator contracts the mean-zero L2 subspace by q<1.
proof_strategy: Use contraction rather than ambiguous gap conventions.
stop_condition: Definition becomes tied to reversibility unnecessarily.
notes: Keep nonreversible absolute contraction separate from Poincare gap.

statement: Discrete path-ordered internal holonomy composes under path concatenation.
proof_strategy: List/Fin ordered-product associativity.
stop_condition: Product order convention is reversed.

statement: Each finite holonomy factor and their ordered product are unitary when the mass/Yukawa generator is Hermitian.
assumptions: Hermitian finite matrices; real step sizes
proof_strategy: Use unitary matrix exponential and closure under products.
stop_condition: Mathlib API does not expose the needed unitary exponential wrapper.
notes: An algebraic Cayley-step discretization is a fallback.

statement: Endpoint gauge transformations conjugate the path-ordered internal holonomy.
assumptions: Gauge field and mass matrix transform compatibly
proof_strategy: Telescoping cancellation across factors.
stop_condition: The gauge action on Yukawa blocks is underspecified.
notes: The theorem charter must name the representation spaces explicitly.

statement: A gauge-invariant relative holonomy controls an actual clock record or transition statistic.
proof_strategy: No proof plan until the physical coupling is specified.
stop_condition: Holonomy remains passive surplus structure.
notes: Do not promote “phase beable is a clock” before this node is replaced by a precise theorem.

statement: A unit Bloch direction maps to the corresponding rank-one qubit projector.
proof_strategy: Reuse banked spin-coherent projector theorem.
stop_condition: Normalization 1/2 is inconsistent.

statement: Any finite positive mixture of product direction projectors is separable.
proof_strategy: Unfold the project definition of separability.
stop_condition: The chosen separability definition is too weak or circular.
notes: Begin with an explicitly finite convex-hull definition.

statement: Every finitely separable multi-qubit state has a finite product-direction representation.
assumptions: Separability defined using pure product qubit states
proof_strategy: Represent each pure qubit ray by a Bloch direction.
stop_condition: Separable decomposition includes mixed local factors.
notes: Either refine the definition or first prove a local spectral decomposition theorem.

statement: An entangled state has no positive local product-null representation.
proof_strategy: Contrapositive of ENT-002.
stop_condition: None.

statement: A named rule assigning hidden-state kernels to quantum states and local updates, with equivariance and covariance contracts.
assumptions: Finite hidden state for the pilot
proof_strategy: Package rule, target equilibrium, and proof obligations.
stop_condition: The rule is so general that every theorem is tautological.
notes: Create concrete substructures: redraw, least-action, Bell-minimal.

statement: Difference between the two hidden kernels around a spacelike update diamond.
proof_strategy: Define two kernel compositions with state-updated rule arguments.
stop_condition: State-update convention is ambiguous.
notes: Store intermediate quantum states explicitly in the definition.

statement: The defect vanishes exactly when the two hidden transports agree extensionally.
proof_strategy: Definition unfolding and kernel extensionality.
stop_condition: None.

statement: On a finite simply connected cut complex, zero diamond defect implies path-independent hidden transport.
assumptions: A presentation of paths by elementary diamond swaps
proof_strategy: Induction over path homotopies / rewriting system.
stop_condition: Diamond moves do not generate all path equivalences.
notes: This is the correct finite analogue of flat synchronization curvature.

statement: Compute the synchronization defect for a Bell pair under a final-state redraw rule.
assumptions: Explicit Bell state and commuting local gates
proof_strategy: Use finite exact matrices and native_decide/norm_num where possible.
stop_condition: The redraw rule is trivially flat and therefore uninformative.
notes: This is a sanity baseline, not the decisive pilot.

statement: Compute the Bell-pair defect for a specified least-action or minimal-transport hidden rule.
assumptions: Concrete hidden state, cost, tie-breaker, and local gates
proof_strategy: Enumerate exact rational/algebraic kernels; compare compositions.
stop_condition: Different admissible rules give different curvature.
notes: The result tests a rule, not entanglement in the abstract.

statement: Under explicit locality, positivity, covariance, and regularity axioms, entanglement forces nonflat hidden transport.
proof_strategy: First search for counterexamples among positive nonlocal flat rules.
stop_condition: A positive flat nonlocal rule is exhibited.
notes: Replace the old unconditional iff conjecture with a quantified theorem-search program.

statement: For self-adjoint H and orthogonal configuration projectors, the finite Bell current is antisymmetric.
proof_strategy: Conjugate symmetry and self-adjointness.
stop_condition: Projector multiplication/order conventions are wrong.
notes: Start with coordinate projectors on a finite basis; abstract Hilbert version later.

statement: If a configuration has zero Born weight, all current terms sourced there vanish.
proof_strategy: Zero norm of projected state implies projected vector is zero.
stop_condition: Born weight is represented only as a real part, obscuring norm zero.
notes: This is the denominator-safety lemma for minimal Bell rates.

statement: Positive-part Bell rates reproduce the finite quantum continuity equation.
proof_strategy: Pair antisymmetric currents and cancel positive/negative parts.
stop_condition: Factor 2/hbar convention mismatch.

statement: If Pq H Pq_prime=0 then the Bell current and minimal rate between q and q_prime vanish.
proof_strategy: Substitute zero block into current; positive part of zero.
stop_condition: None.

statement: A finite Fock-sector Bell generator plus destination direction laws preserves the extended Born law.
proof_strategy: Treat sector change as a base jump and apply the generic lift.
stop_condition: Creation direction laws are not measurable/normalized in the chosen API.
notes: Forward equation only; actual continuous-time path existence is separate.

statement: A bounded finite time-dependent Bell generator determines a nonexplosive càdlàg jump process.
proof_strategy: Construct exponential waiting times and embedded jump chain, or import a future CTMC API.
stop_condition: Path construction becomes a standalone probability formalization.
notes: Not needed for the discrete-time Gate G1 model.

statement: A relation classifying allowed propagation, local internal flips, and local creation/annihilation events.
proof_strategy: Separate propagation edges from same-vertex internal events in an inductive predicate.
stop_condition: The relation labels local flips as “null motion” ambiguously.
notes: The ontology claim should say spacetime-changing continuations are null; internal events have zero displacement.

statement: Operator block support on primitive continuations implies Bell current/rate support on those continuations.
proof_strategy: Direct support transfer.
stop_condition: None; intentionally simple.

statement: The support of D^2 is contained in the two-step relational closure of the support of D.
assumptions: Finite matrix D supported on relation R
proof_strategy: Expand matrix multiplication and witness an intermediate state.
stop_condition: Cancellation can make support smaller, never larger.
notes: This corrects the misconception that a local first-order operator has a nearest-neighbor square.

statement: The concrete 1+1 checkerboard first-order step operator has exactly null propagation support plus local coin mixing.
proof_strategy: Inspect matrix blocks for shift and coin factors.
stop_condition: One-step unitary mixes coin and shift so block support is misclassified.
notes: This is the first non-tautological operator-support instance.

statement: The graph super-Dirac commutator with scalar multiplication has local symbol equal to a weighted null slash.
assumptions: Concrete order-complex operator, edge spinors, soldering map
proof_strategy: Construct D first; compute [D,Mf]; identify coefficients with edge momenta.
stop_condition: No natural coefficient map produces the null slash.
notes: This is the central interpretation-to-theory gate.

statement: The squared local null symbol equals the weighted Pluecker mass scalar.
proof_strategy: Compose the symbol identification with the static square-root theorem.
stop_condition: Weights or momentum aggregation differ from the banked theorem.

statement: The concrete causal super-Dirac operator has only null propagation and local internal-event support.
proof_strategy: Inspect each block: covariant differential, codifferential, Higgs/Yukawa.
stop_condition: Covariant codifferential or effective causal-set term is nonlocal.
notes: A failure here means the graph theory no longer has strictly null primitive spacetime continuations.

statement: The abstract block square decomposes into Laplacian, curvature/cross, and Higgs/Yukawa blocks.
proof_strategy: Audit and wrap existing algebra; no new physics claim.
stop_condition: Existing theorem double-counts kinetic and Yukawa mass.
notes: On-shell equality is a constraint; determinant and Phi†Phi are not additive mass terms.

statement: Each candidate operator is classified by null support, intrinsic locality, retardedness, covariance in law, stability, and continuum convergence.
proof_strategy: Formalize properties separately; do not assume a blanket no-go.
stop_condition: The property vocabulary cannot compare regular graphs and causal sets coherently.
notes: Current literature includes both established nonlocal operators and a recent local proposal, so this must remain an audit.

statement: An enlarged hidden-state first-order operator with null support whose projected square realizes an effective operator.
proof_strategy: Define embed, project, lifted D, support, and realization equation.
stop_condition: The realization equation is too weak and admits trivial encodings.
notes: Add dimensional, locality, covariance, and naturality constraints before interpreting a positive result.

statement: A selected continuum-correct causal operator admits a natural covariant null-local dilation.
assumptions: A concrete causal-set box and naturality axioms
proof_strategy: Search finite truncations first; formulate either construction or no-go.
stop_condition: Only artificial state-space blowups satisfy the definition.
notes: This is a sharper open question than “local lattice versus nonlocal causal set”.

statement: A named L2/Poincare or semigroup-contraction certificate with explicit invariant measure and constant.
proof_strategy: Separate reversible Poincare gap, absolute contraction, and time-dependent mixing.
stop_condition: One definition is stretched across incompatible settings.
notes: Use multiple structures rather than one overloaded “gap”.

statement: In an abstract Hilbert space, a bounded coercive bilinear form has a unique weak solution.
assumptions: Complete real inner-product space; coercive form
proof_strategy: Wrap mathlib Lax-Milgram with the project’s source/zero-mean notation.
stop_condition: The wrapper accidentally claims a sphere PDE instance.
notes: This can be completed now and cleanly separates library theorem from geometric instantiation.

statement: The weighted angular Poisson problem on S^2 has a unique zero-mean weak solution under uniform positive bounds.
assumptions: Sobolev space on sphere; Poincare inequality; bounded weight
proof_strategy: Instantiate Lax-Milgram on a quotient/zero-mean Sobolev space.
stop_condition: Required analysis is absent or too costly for the physics payoff.
notes: Finite graph least action remains the certified substitute.

statement: For homogeneous rotational diffusion on S^2, l=1 direction correlation decays as exp(-2Ds).
assumptions: A constructed sphere diffusion semigroup and coordinate eigenfunction theorem
proof_strategy: Prove coordinate functions are eigenfunctions of the generator, then semigroup action.
stop_condition: No formal diffusion process or generator exists in the project.
notes: Do not name the theorem “Compton locked” until D=alpha m is separately assumed or derived.

statement: The Dirac/Yukawa mass operator fixes the angular mixing scale D=alpha m with a derived alpha.
assumptions: A microscopic operator coupling hidden direction to chirality
proof_strategy: Derive generator from operator rather than postulate it.
stop_condition: Alpha remains arbitrary or state-dependent.
notes: This is the real mass-as-dynamics claim.

statement: The state-dependent sphere process exists globally and is nonexplosive away from named singular sets.
assumptions: Regularity, ellipticity, bounded coefficients, node avoidance
proof_strategy: Use SDE/martingale-problem theory after infrastructure exists.
stop_condition: The assumptions exclude physically important nodes/massless limits.
notes: State the excluded set and stopping-time version explicitly.

statement: There exists a finite model whose every primitive increment is null, whose position law is exactly specified, and whose single realized coarse velocity converges almost surely to a prescribed timelike U.
assumptions: Finite null resolution and iid sampling
proof_strategy: Package octahedral resolution, product trajectory law, nullity, and strong law.
mathlib_support: PMF; Ionescu-Tulcea or product measure; StrongLaw
stop_condition: The model is only kinematic and is mislabeled as Bohm-Bell dynamics.
notes: Call it the finite IID null-strand model; it establishes the ontology/coarse-graining possibility cleanly.

statement: The 1+1 lattice model has unitary wave evolution, exact Born equivariance, an actual trajectory, and null motion at every tick.
assumptions: Banked checkerboard dynamics; ZZ-006 through ZZ-009; trajectory measure
proof_strategy: Package the exact discrete model.
stop_condition: Infinite trajectory construction is omitted.
notes: This is the first genuinely pilot-wave/Bohmian capstone.

statement: Any finite causal base process with a chosen null resolution admits an exact equivariant hidden null-direction lift.
assumptions: Base forward equation; positive finite resolutions; residual compatibility
proof_strategy: Package the generic lift and marginal theorem; pathwise mixing remains separate.
stop_condition: The theorem is presented as a unique dynamics rather than an unravelling family.
notes: State nonuniqueness explicitly in the theorem commentary.

statement: A covariant one-particle continuum process has null sample paths, the correct flux/Born marginal, and a pathwise timelike coarse limit.
assumptions: MEA-003; MEA-006; CONT-005; a spectral/mixing certificate
proof_strategy: Assemble measure, process existence, equivariance, and ergodic theorem.
stop_condition: Any hypothesis simply assumes the desired process or mixing.
notes: Keep this theorem conditional with every analytic assumption visible.

statement: Given hypersurface continuity, conditional-current resolutions, jump-process existence, and mixing, the many-particle foliated theory is equivariant with null spacetime-changing tangents.
assumptions: Explicit foliation; multi-time wavefunction; analytic certificates
proof_strategy: Build only after the finite Fock model and synchronization framework are stable.
stop_condition: The theorem hides the foliation as an “assumption” without specifying its covariance law.
notes: A fixed or state-generated foliation must be a named input structure.

statement: A concrete first-order graph operator with null support, correct square, and controlled scaling induces null-supported Bell histories and the desired continuum observables.
assumptions: GRAPH-004 through GRAPH-009 plus Bell current machinery
proof_strategy: Assemble only after one concrete operator passes every audit.
mathlib_support: Finite operator algebra plus external scaling theory
stop_condition: The operator cannot satisfy support, covariance, and continuum correctness simultaneously.
notes: Only this gate upgrades the program from an interpretation/unravelling to candidate new dynamics.
